failure_modes.text: Unwarranted optimism can make a multi-step plan look safer than the compounded odds justify.
failure_modes.note: The raw risk table names the failure mode directly; the chunk expands it into a conjunctive-plan failure statement.
failure_modes.mitigation: Decompose the multi-step plan into individual probabilities and compute the joint: the compounded odds are almost always lower than the narrative makes them feel.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on one plausible step can hide how fragile the full sequence really is.
failure_modes.note: Preserves the raw risk-table item while tying it to sequence-level overconfidence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Challenge anchoring on the most plausible single step by asking what must also go right for the full sequence to hold.

failure_modes.text: Lazy System 1 acceptance lets a smooth narrative substitute for explicit probability discipline.
failure_modes.mitigation: Force explicit probability discipline: require the team to assign and multiply probabilities rather than accepting a smooth narrative as evidence of likelihood.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What specific assumptions must all hold for this chain to succeed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.note: The raw source uses the question as a challenge device; the chunk specializes it to conjunctive plans.

premortem_questions.text: Which step breaks first if one dependency slips, and what happens to the rest of the plan then?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you're doing solve the problem? How does it advance your thinking?
premortem_questions.note: This is a conservative operational normalization from the raw mitigation prompts and sequence-risk focus.

heuristics.text: Use reference class forecasting before trusting the inside story of a multi-step success case.
Run worst-case scrutiny on the chain instead of only arguing from the happy path.
Decompose the plan into MECE steps so hidden dependencies and stacked assumptions become inspectable.

curation_notes.summary: The raw file is strong on sequential failure risk and named mitigation frameworks, so the chunk set stays close to those sources.
donor_drops: Did not carry donor routing-style scenario examples because this layer is intervention-semantics only.
open_questions: One premortem question is still a reviewed normalization because the raw file names challenge prompts more than complete sequence-failure questions.

allies: Raw allies material is strong on outside-view and probability-oriented support models that could matter in a later relation wave.
antagonists: Raw antagonists and risk rows are rich on optimism, anchoring, and motivated-reasoning conflicts.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension block was preserved here, but the raw risk table suggests future tension work around optimism and narrative plausibility.
